title: Calculating Intersection over Union (IoU)
concept: Coding the IoU function to measure overlap between predicted boxes and ground-truth boxes. 'Next up: CV Project: Fine-Tuning.'.

exercises.prompt: What do anchor boxes help us to do in object detection?

options.text: They define candidate aspect ratios and scales.
They create the final detected image.

exercises.explain: Anchor boxes are used to define candidate aspect ratios and scales for detecting objects.

cards.title: Understanding IoU
cards.body: Intersection over Union (IoU) measures how well two boxes overlap. It's crucial for determining the accuracy of object detection.

cards.title: What is IoU?
cards.body: IoU is a value between 0 and 1 that shows the overlap of predicted and ground-truth bounding boxes. A higher IoU means a better match.

cards.title: Think of Overlapping Shadows
cards.body: Imagine casting shadows of two overlapping objects. The area where the shadows overlap represents their IoU, indicating how well they cover the same space.

cards.title: Calculating IoU Example
cards.body: If Box A overlaps with Box B by an area of 4 and their combined area is 10, the IoU is 4/10 = 0.4. This shows a moderate overlap.

cards.title: IoU Doesn't Measure Size
cards.body: Some may think IoU accounts for the size of the boxes, but it only considers the area of overlap versus total area.

cards.title: Visualizing IoU
cards.body: The left box shows the predicted bounding box and the right shows the ground-truth box. The shaded area represents the intersection used to calculate IoU.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes how to calculate IoU?

options.text: Add the areas of both boxes together.
Divide the area of overlap by the total area of both boxes.
Subtract the area of one box from the other.
Multiply the lengths of the boxes together.

exercises.explain: To calculate IoU, you divide the area of overlap by the total area covered by both boxes, giving you a measure of how well they match.

exercises.prompt: IoU can be a value greater than 1.
exercises.explain: IoU is always a value between 0 and 1; thus, it cannot exceed 1.

exercises.prompt: The value of IoU ranges from __________ to 1.
exercises.sentence: The value of IoU ranges from 0 to 1.
exercises.explain: IoU measures overlap and is defined to be between the minimum of 0 (no overlap) and the maximum of 1 (perfect overlap).

exercises.prompt: Which of the following represents a correct code implementation for IoU calculation?
exercises.explain: The correct formula ensures both the intersection area and the union of both boxes are accurately represented in the calculation.

exercises.prompt: What does a higher IoU score indicate about the predicted bounding box?

options.text: The prediction is better aligned with ground-truth.
The prediction has a larger area than ground-truth.
The prediction box is completely different.
The prediction is less reliable than ground-truth.

exercises.explain: A higher IoU score indicates a better match between the predicted bounding box and the ground-truth box, showing that the detection is accurate and reliable. Next up: CV Project: Fine-Tuning.
